judge_reason: The descriptions contradict each other on the specific verbatim text of a stamp: A reads 'JAN 2 5 1977' and 'WAITED TO', while B reads 'WARDED TO JAN 3 1977'.
original_image_tags: Aged, pale yellow document page, torn at the top edge with dark holes suggesting it was previously stapled or filed. The page is largely blank with faint, illegible text showing through from the reverse side. There is a red rectangular stamp near the bottom right containing the text "JAN 2 5 1977" and "WAITED TO". In the bottom left, there is a handwritten annotation in dark ink: "JAN 21 3 57 PM '77". Above this is a stamped line: "REC'D CORRESPONDENCE REVIEW F B I". In the lower left margin, a vertical red stamp reads "JAN 2 8 1977". The entire page appears to be a back or routing sheet with no primary text content visible on this side.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A scanned, mostly blank yellowed document page with torn and clipped edges, faint reversed text showing through, and two faint red/purple rubber-stamp markings near the lower center and lower left.*
